next_action: Session 70: The proof path is now: Euler product Gaussianization (formal group, arXiv:2602.20211) forces xi to be near-Gaussian. Gaussian IS LP. Show the corrections (higher cumulants) preserve LP at all scales. Four angles converge: GORZ Hermite, formal group, our F/xi decomp, misaligned SUSY. The key computation: verify cumulant decay rate kappa_{2k} is fast enough to satisfy Jensen hyperbolicity at ALL (d,n), not just large n. This connects formal group theory to the GORZ program. Paper is at v5 (11 pages, 14 sections).
context_notes: Session went from white flag to proof path in one sitting. Key discoveries: (1) Z=2*Voros Z_1(2) identified, (2) Turan inequalities all pass, (3) F fails d>=3 but xi passes, (4) LP is 100% arithmetic, (5) Euler product Gaussianization IS the mechanism (four angles). The d=2 Turan was already proved (CNV 1986). The real frontier: d>=3 at small shifts. 200 commits on main branch.
